title: Rich Tender Russian Lamb Stew
description: Cultured, molten, and deliciously post-workout, this Russian lamb stew is a comforting delight featuring dijon mustard and lacto-vegetarian-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat the Infuse-Oil in a kadhai over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the lamb from the kadhai and set aside.
Add the sliced onions to the kadhai and cook until they are softened and lightly browned, about 10 minutes.
Add the sliced carrots and potatoes to the kadhai and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, Dijon mustard, salt, and ground black pepper to the kadhai and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red wine to the kadhai and cook until the liquid has almost completely evaporated, about 5 minutes.
Add the beef broth to the kadhai and stir to combine.
Return the lamb to the kadhai and bring the stew to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the fresh thyme and serve hot.
Let the stew rest for 10-15 minutes before serving.
